premise: Prove LAF can reason over its OWN generated ERP product (LAF-EnterpriseERP-Gold), not just store documents. Answered by the deterministic CodeGraph + KnowledgeIndex + ExperienceMemory; no model required.

questions.result: AppUser, UserAuthService, AccountController and their dependents located
auth test classes that reference the service are returned via TestCovers edges
ManufacturingService located with its file path

questions.q: What is impacted if StockLedgerEntry changes?
What reports depend on the ledger?
What knowledge packs were created from ERP depth?
What generator template reproduces a generated file?

questions.result: controllers, services, dbcontext, and entities enumerated from the real Gold graph

questions.q: Why did the LocalDB migration fail earlier?
questions.result: PendingModelChangesWarning -> Gold has LLM modules absent from the committed migration -> apply from the deterministic emit

questions.q: What is needed to push ERP parity from 45% to 55%?
questions.result: HR/payroll/POS/e-commerce flows + delivery-note/return chains + batch/serial stock (from the depth gap matrix)

honest: Reasoning is deterministic retrieval over a syntax-only graph: references are by simple name, so precise call graphs and dynamic dispatch are not modelled; a few questions (e.g. exact Playwright-to-report coverage) are answered via knowledge/file mapping rather than the call graph. An optional local model would only enrich the explanations, not the retrieval.
